title: Ensure IAM users receive permissions only through groups

input_row.title: Ensure IAM users receive permissions only through groups
input_row.description: IAM users are granted access to services, functions, and data through IAM policies. There are four ways to define policies for a user: 1) Edit the user policy directly, also known as an inline or user policy; 2) attach a policy directly to a user; 3) add the user to an IAM group that has an attached policy; 4) add the user to an IAM group that has an inline policy. Only the third implementation is recommended.
input_row.rationale: Assigning IAM policies solely through groups unifies permissions management into a single, flexible layer that is consistent with organizational functional roles. By unifying permissions management, the likelihood of excessive permissions is reduced.
input_row.audit: Perform the following to determine if an inline policy is set or a policy is directly attached to users: 1. Run the following to get a list of IAM users: aws iam list-users --query 'Users[*].UserName' --output text 2. For each user returned, run the following command to determine if any policies are attached to them: aws iam list-attached-user-policies --user-name <iam_user> aws iam list-user-policies --user-name <iam_user> 3. If any policies are returned, the user has an inline policy or direct policy attachment.
input_row.remediation: Perform the following to create an IAM group and assign a policy to it: Internal Only - General 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the IAM console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/. 2. In the navigation pane, click Groups and then click Create New Group. 3. In the Group Name box, type the name of the group and then click Next Step. 4. In the list of policies, select the check box for each policy that you want to apply to all members of the group. Then click Next Step. 5. Click Create Group. Perform the following to add a user to a given group: 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the IAM console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/. 2. In the navigation pane, click Groups. 3. Select the group to add a user to. 4. Click Add Users To Group. 5. Select the users to be added to the group. 6. Click Add Users. Perform the following to remove a direct association between a user and policy: 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the IAM console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/. 2. In the left navigation pane, click on Users. 3. For each user: o Select the user o Click on the Permissions tab o Expand Permissions policies o Click X for each policy; then click Detach or Remove (depending on policy type)

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_iam_user_permissions_via_groups_only

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **AWS API Calls / Boto3 SDK Methods:**
   - Use `boto3.client('iam')` to create an IAM client and perform the following actions:
     - `list_users()` to retrieve a list of IAM users.
     - For each user, use `list_attached_user_policies(UserName=<iam_user>)` to check for attached policies and `list_user_policies(UserName=<iam_user>)` for inline policies.

2. **AWS CLI Commands:**
   - `aws iam list-users --query 'Users[*].UserName' --output text` to get a list of IAM users.
   - For each user:
     - `aws iam list-attached-user-policies --user-name <iam_user>` to check for attached policies.
     - `aws iam list-user-policies --user-name <iam_user>` to check for inline policies.

3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - IAM users across all AWS accounts in the region(s) of interest.

4. **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Check if any IAM user has either inline policies or directly attached policies.

5. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass:** If no inline policies or directly attached policies are found for each IAM user.
   - **Fail:** If any inline policies or directly attached policies are identified for any IAM user.

6. **Example Check:**
   - If `list_user_policies()` or `list_attached_user_policies()` returns any results for a user, that user is not compliant with the control.
